stops.name: JJ Bean
stops.description: Local legend cafe. Home of my favorite chocolate chip cookie. Local meet up spot with decent little patio in the back, great drinks and an excellent roast of espresso.
stops.goto: A shot of espresso and a chocolate chunk cookie or a krispie oatmeal raising cookie.

stops.name: Rollzzy
stops.description: Your favorite Indian dishes, wrapped up in a delicious package to go. Both meat and veggie options.
stops.goto: Chicken Tikka roll

stops.name: Prado Cafe
stops.description: Locally owned coffee shop with great coffee and some unique, hearty baked goods. Great spot to sit on the corner and people watch.
stops.goto: An americano and a "cookie with no name" sitting outside on the south facing bench.

stops.name: Skewers Souvlaki Pita Bar
stops.description: Newer locally owned spot with super authentic Souvlaki. Big share plates or personal portions.
stops.goto: Lamb gyro wrap.

stops.name: La Grotta del Formaggio
stops.description: Classic Italian Deli with a delicious sandwich counter and quick stop for European groceries.
stops.goto: I mostly go here for hard-to-find Italian groceries, but everyone loves the make your own sandwich bar.

stops.name: Belli Pizza
stops.description: Newest pizza place to hit Commercial. Their dough is super high quality with a unique flavor, they cook it to perfection with a simple menu that has me coming back for more. The late night vibe is worth checking out as their playlists are always on point and the drinks somehow always keep on flowing.
stops.goto: The Fennel Sausage or Hawaiian pizza would be my first choices, with a glass of lambrusco.

stops.name: Mintage
stops.description: One of the oldest vintage sellers in the city. Amazing selection of all types of vintage, from one of a kind dresses, to ratty band tees, beloved sports jackets, rocks and gems, reworks, and so much more. Worth a look even if you're not shopping for anything.
stops.goto: I often go in here to check out the jackets and sweaters in the back right corner.

stops.name: Loula's
stops.description: Upscale Greek dishes with authentic flavors. Great cocktails and a fun evening vibe if you're into staying late. Check out the backroom for more cocktails.
stops.goto: The dips and octops are usually my starter and ifI'm feelin hungry, I'll usualy go with the paidakia (lamb chops)

stops.name: Bar Corso
stops.description: Good late afternoon - late night Italian snacks and cocktails. Cute little patio on the sidewalk with a good mix of wine and cocktails on the menu.
stops.goto: A cocktail and an order of meatballs on the patio on a warm summer evening.

stops.name: The Charlatan
stops.description: A neighborhood staple that takes in all walks of life. A big sidewalk facing patio where you can catch some evening sun, a cold beer, some wings, and potentially some live jazz. Inside has screens for all your sport-viewing needs and often has a good crew of locals kicking around.
stops.goto: Wing night is usually when you'll find me at the Charlatan, with a pint of guiness, some friends, and too many orders of wings.

stops.name: Cozen Japanese Cuisine
stops.description: My goto sushi spot on Commercial. A bit more expensive compared to some of the other options, but I've never been disappointed in the quality of their fish and their Japanese dishes.
stops.goto: Salmon Oshi, Miso Soup, Inari negiri, black lager.

stops.name: Alterior
stops.description: Alterior is a local clothing manufacturer specializing in denim goods. They carry a bunch of specially curated brands, have tonnes of pop-ups, and hidden corners of the shop include Book Section upstairs. This space is part clothing store, part manufacturing studio, part art space. A great place to meet locals and learn about the neighborhood.
stops.goto: DENIM.

stops.name: Vintage Sponsor
stops.description: Hidden inside Alterior, Vintage Sponsor is the legendary vintage dealer whose collection is always growing and changing. I buy something almost every time I am in this shop. He has a wide range of styles from different eras and his eye for quality pieces is unlike any other shop in the city. With occasional pop-up events, and a wealth of knowledge, it's always worth popping in to see what's new.
stops.goto: T-shirts, dress shirts, polos

stops.name: LIVIA Forno e Vino
stops.description: One of the cutest coffee shops in the neighborhood. Great patio with an amazing piazza next door where you can sit with your take out if the restaurant is too busy (it usually is!). Amazing baked goods made in house every day, a great place for a coffee, a treat, lunch, or to take home some bread.
stops.goto: Breakfast sandwich with mortadella and an americano

stops.name: Mediterranean Specialty Foods - Turkish Market
stops.description: A great place to stop to get some Mediterranean groceries or a quick snack.
stops.goto: feta dip and a baked pie to go.

stops.name: Sula Indian Restaurant
stops.description: With multiple locations in the city, it's no secret that this is some of the best Indian food you can get. I've tried many of the dishes and always find myself coming back for more!
stops.goto: Share platter to start, then some dal makhani, saag paneer, butter chicken and lamb vindaloo.

stops.name: Nomo Nomo
stops.description: A newer Izakaya on the block, this location has some of the best cocktails in the city. Some serious chemistry going on in here to get some wild cocktails. Their super sharable plates are awesome for dining with 2-6 people, make a reso, this place fills up fast!
stops.goto: Wagyu sliders and a tonka cocktail

stops.name: Magari by Oca
stops.description: One of the most in demand restaurants in the city. Unrivaled hand-made pasta that truly provides a unique tasting-menu experience. The team there is super knowledgeable and always a great time. Get in line by 4:30 if you want to be in the first round of seating!
stops.goto: Tasting menu.

stops.name: Don't Argue Pizza
stops.description: A great local spot to grab a slice, a shot of espresso, and catch a game. One of the best slices in the city hands down and one of my go-tos for a quick bite. Authentic NY slice in the heart of little Italy.
stops.goto: Daily special slice or just a simple slice of pepperoni.

stops.name: Sweet Cherubim
stops.description: A long-standing Commercial drive staple. This is part restaurant part grocery shop. My go to here is a Potato Pea fried Samosa and Chocolate Macaroon cookie to go. Large selection of natural foods, drinks, vitamins, and much more. The other side of the shop is a sit-down restaurant with some super tasty traditional dishes.
stops.goto: Spinach feta fried samosa or potato pea.

stops.name: Mogu Fried Chicken
stops.description: Korean Fried chicken on a bowl of rice with pickles. Enough said.
stops.goto: Fried chicken on rice bowl.

stops.name: Downlow Chicken Shack
stops.description: A local favorite, Downlow always serves amazing southern style fried chicken. I usually go for the mild options, but if you're feeling spicy, there is a ton of options to get your sweat on.
stops.goto: I always go for the chicken tenders, sometimes spicy, sometimes plain

stops.name: Kin Kao
stops.description: Wonderful, cozy, Thai restaurant with some amazing curries and pad-thai dishes. Menu changes frequently and I love checking in at least once a year to see whats new.
stops.goto: Stir fry pork belly

stops.name: Caffè La Tana
stops.description: One of my forever favorite Cafes in the city. Their lunch is always fantastic, stop for a coffee and a quick bite with rotating pastas, salads, and sandwiches to satisfy any taste for any time of day.
stops.goto: I am a regular buyer of their big bag of coffee, I love the roast. If I'm there and hungry I usually go for the kale salad and breakfast sandwich.

stops.name: FUN HÄUS
stops.description: One of the newest Vintage shops in the neighborhood by two OG vintage pickers in the city. You can find anything your vintage heart desires from super accessible collectables and clothes all the way up to rare t-shirts that have back stories that will make your head spin.
stops.goto: Puruse the toys with my daughter, hear a story from Kirby or Andy, and look through all the t-shirts!

stops.name: Slice of Life Art Gallery
stops.description: Part art-gallery, part shop, part co-working space, photo booth, part vintage shop this place has it all. It is a must stop if you make it to Venables from the skytrain, there is truly something for everyone. Local gifts made by local artists of all shapes and sizes.
stops.goto: Look through the stickers, art books, head to the vintage shop, then photo booth before taking in what's in the gallery that week.

stops.name: Audiopile
stops.description: A huge selection of used and new records, one of the OG spots on the drive for all your music needs.
stops.goto: Flip through the previously owned section!

stops.name: Canterbury Tales
stops.description: A quaint little book shop where you can find used and new books from many different genres.
stops.goto: Wander around the front to see the new suggested books.

stops.name: The Drive Skateshop
stops.description: A great spot to check out some local and legendary brands, magazines, equipment, hardware, or anything skateboard related!
stops.goto: Chat with the local skaters who work there and check out whats new on the deck wall in the back.

stops.name: Bump N Grind Cafe
stops.description: An eccelectic little coffee shop at the end of the drive with the only clover coffee machine in the neighborhood. They also have other flavored drinks, if that's your vibe!
stops.goto: Clover coffee and a reese pieces cookie

stops.name: Book Section
stops.description: Tucked away upstairs in Alterior, book section is a free library where you can browse Donnel's (local photographer) amazing collection of magazines and books.
stops.goto: Flip through whatever magazines/books Donnel has put up under the window.

stops.name: Mah Milk Bar
stops.description: One block east of Commercial, Mah is a great place to grab a coffee and light lunch, or some unique groceries and sit on their little patio, or head across the street into the park to enjoy your purchases.
stops.goto: Morning glory muffin

stops.name: Victoria Park
stops.description: Nice little park to play with the kids, throw down a blanket, or just sit on a bench while you are between Commercial and Victoria.
stops.goto: Grab a coffee and treat from Mah and set up at the playground with my kiddo or on the grass near the lavendar.

stops.name: Grandview Park
stops.description: Big park in the heart of Commercial, places to sit and have mountain views, a splash pad and great playground. Locals can be boisterous here, but they are usually a welcoming group. Great spot for people watching or eating a slice of pizza, sneaky beer, or coffee.
stops.goto: Grab a samosa or slice of pizza and watch the sunset.

stops.name: Havana
stops.description: One of the best patios in the neighborhood, lots of space and great people watching. Food is good, drinks are cold, and vibes are high.
stops.goto: Pitcher of sangria or margs on the patio. Cubano or fish tacos if you're hungry.

stops.name: Easy Shop
stops.description: My go to stop for a quick drink, snack, or just to say hi to the gentleman who work there!
stops.goto: Only spot on the drive that has sugar free orangina!

stops.name: Mi Tierra Latina Store
stops.description: If you're looking for latinx snacks, this is your go to stop! This shop is filled with authentic groceries from all over North, Central, and South America. The best part is, in the back corner, are cheap and delicious tacos. One of the best kept secrets in the neighborhood (you're lucky I'm tellin you!).
stops.goto: Tacos in the back!

stops.name: Bench Bakery
stops.description: My wifes favorite cinnamon bun in the city, great sausage roll and cookies too!
stops.goto: Sausage roll (and/or)  cookie (and/or) cinnamon bun.

stops.name: Flourist Bakery
stops.description: A great spot for coffee, snacks, or a full lunch. Some of the best sourdough in the city and a great spot to sit in the sun. It's further South from the Skytrain than the main Commercial Drive area, but it's definitely worth a visit.
stops.goto: Coffee, cookie, daily quiche or beans on toast.

stops.name: Equinox Gallery
stops.description: One of my favorite galleries in the entire city with a constant rotating showing.
stops.goto: Grab a coffee from Flourist and head over to walk through the gallery.

stops.name: Earnest Ice Cream
stops.description: Top 3 Ice cream in the city hands down. I could go for a scoop of Cookies & Cream any day. A bit off the beaten path, but close to a big park with a playground and a large open field where locals tend to picnic.
stops.goto: Sample everything but get a kids cone of cookies and cream.

stops.name: Woodland Park
stops.description: A secret gem in the neighborhood. With a concrete wading pool that gets filled during the day it's a great place for parents and kids to cool off in the summer. The playground is "oldschool", but still provides lots of fun. You will often find locals picnicking in the large open fields, a great place to also watch all the bikes zip past the Adanac bike path. With three local breweries a stone's throw away, it's an easy choice for a chill afternoon/evening hang out.
stops.goto: Cool off in the concrete wading pool in the summer. Grab a six-pack from Bomber and chill on the benches. Great spot to eat your cone form Earnest.

stops.name: Bomber Brewing
stops.description: A great place to grab a pint, a taco from the food truck, or grab a six pack to drink in the park. Also check out their selection of sparkling water!
stops.goto: Pack of courts water and a six pack of lager and head to the park with a blanket.

stops.name: Superflux
stops.description: One of my favorite breweries in the city. Some of the freshest beers you can find, with lots of rotating taps, off sales, and a great kitchen serving up more than just "pub food".
stops.goto: ICY COOL or NITRO STOUT

stops.name: Strange Fellows
stops.description: The home of "old world beer" with some of the most pungent and flavorful beers you'll have. With a patio and classic beer snacks, this place is a go to for a strong pint!
stops.goto: A dark beer and some bar snacks in the winter.

stops.name: Mums the Word
stops.description: This place has a south facing patio that keeps you nice and shaded in the hot summer months while still being able to people watch with a mountain view. Coffee and cocktails to either start or end your day!
stops.goto: Shot of espresso in the morning, cold brew Manhattan at night.

stops.name: Hackspace Vancouver (Mini Cinema)
stops.description: A mini free cinema on the west wall at Vancouver Hack Space, push the button for curated short films, open 24/7. Find the fourth window from the alley along Woodland.
stops.goto: Check out the mini cinema to see what's playing.

stops.name: The Burrow
stops.description: East Van's go-to for vegetarian and vegan Mexican — tacos, burritos, nachos, and a standout weekend brunch in a warm, neighbourhood-room kind of space. Same kitchen you may remember as Bandidas, with a name that fits the community vibe.
stops.goto: Burrow breakfast burrito.

stops.name: St. Augustine's
stops.description: If you're wanting to stay close to the skytrain and taste a bunch of local beer's this is your spot. The pizza and nachos are good too!
stops.goto: Stout and nachos

stops.name: Gatley
stops.description: A neighborhood lifestyle shop since 2012 with women's clothing, household goods, and everyday objects. Locally owned and great stop for gifts!
stops.goto: Bagu bags, soaps, and my book!

stops.name: The Courts
stops.description: The local legend DIY skateboard spot. Come by here at any time of day to see locals of all skill levels skating. A welcoming atmosphere, with community events sprinkled throughout the year.
stops.goto: Early mornings with a coffee and spine sessions.

stops.name: Truck Stop Cafe
stops.description: If you're looking for a long standing, greasy spoon, this is your spot. Great food, diverse menu, and homestyle meals.
stops.goto: Pancakes and a coffee

stops.name: Fujiya - Clark Drive
stops.description: This Japanese grocer is a great spot to try some new snacks, grab a cheap quality lunch, or get some groceries.
stops.goto: Inari negiri, a random drink, and crunchy snack

stops.name: Kasko Frame Works
stops.description: Kasko Frame Works is usually a custom framing store, but often, the front of the room is an art gallery, pop your head in and say hello and see what's up

stops.name: Bosa Foods
stops.description: Bosa is a local grocer specializing in Italian goods. Stop in for a sandwich or something from the hot table. Also a good option to grab some euro groceries if you are planning on cooking at home.
stops.goto: Lupini, biscotti, and a meatball sandwich

stops.name: Butter & Jazz Vintage + Thrift
stops.description: One of the newest vintage spaces in the neighborhood, this spot has a very cool vibe and a great selection of vintage and thrifted items.

stops.name: Sneaky Pete's Pool Café
stops.description: A modern pool cafe with a full service bar, lots of tables, and some food options.
stops.goto: Pint and a few games.

stops.name: Yama Cafe
stops.description: Cute little cafe on Hastings to get a quick snack and a coffee before heading down to see what else this neighborhood has in store.
stops.goto: A coffee and a couple moffins.

stops.name: Pallet Coffee Roasters - Semlin Drive
stops.description: Pallet is a local roaster with a stellar line-up. They always have great offerings and the patio is a nice place to stop before heading further into the neighborhood.
stops.goto: Some of the best roasts in the city.

stops.name: Niwa Restaurant
stops.description: A farm to table restaurant from a legendary local. If you are looking for a refined dining experience, this is your stop. Great selection of wine, sake, and unique cocktails. Super fresh dishes and an amazing "chefs selection" dining option.
stops.goto: Chefs selection, always.

stops.name: Parallel 49 Brewing Company
stops.description: P49 is a fun and accessible brewery that offers lots of patio seating, great beers, and fun bar food. They have live music in the summer and plenty of seating for large groups. Tons of great beers to try as well.
stops.goto: Trash panda, nitro stout, waffle fries.

stops.name: Pandora Park
stops.description: Activities for adults and kids Pandora is steps away from E Hastings and has lots to offer.
stops.goto: Grab lunch or ice cream from a spot on Hastings and hang out.

stops.name: East End Billiards
stops.description: An old school billiards hall. You'll find some real locals in this spot.

stops.name: East Café
stops.description: Describing themselves as a "true third place", the East Cafe prides themselves on being a welcoming, grounding, and creative place to hang out in the neighborhood.
stops.goto: Coffee and check out the art.

stops.name: Blind Rabbit
stops.description: A unique local bar that specializes in gin cocktails and charcuterie boards.
stops.goto: Dirty gin martini and a couple of snacks.

stops.name: Tamam: Fine Palestinian Cuisine
stops.description: Authentic Palestinian food with amazing flavors. Lots of veggie and meat options on the menu to satisfy any needs.
stops.goto: Lam roast with mujaddarah.

stops.name: Tacofino Commissary
stops.description: One of many Tacofino locations, the commissary offers some of the usual Tacofino options with a few local specific options as well.
stops.goto: Love taking advantage of the burrito-to-bowl option when I am dining in.

stops.name: Tall Shadow Bakery
stops.description: A local bakery specializing in sourdough baked goods. With daily (Weds-Sun) offerings and daily specific offerings, there is always something great to grab from this spot.
stops.goto: Jalapeno & Cheddar sourdough (Fridays!).

stops.name: It's Okay
stops.description: A fun spot to grab a cocktail and a burger and hang out with friends. Good vibes and good drinks.
stops.goto: It's okay bar mix, pickles, and a pina colada.

stops.name: Pantitlán
stops.description: The newest, most popular taco spot in the city. If you don't mind waiting, this former hidden gem, tucked away behind a Mexican bakery, serves some authentic Mexican cocktails and tacos.
stops.goto: pastor and suedero with a marg and beer chaser.

stops.name: Dachi Restaurant and Natural Wine
stops.description: An intimate dining experience with super knowledgable staff, unique dishes, and great selection of natural wine and sake.
stops.goto: Share tasting menu

stops.name: Doe Coffee Roasters
stops.description: Doe is a newer coffee roaster in the neighborhood. They offer an "all you can drink" membership option which is quite unique. Their baked goods are exceptional and their service is always friendly.
stops.goto: Muffin and a coffee.

stops.name: Jackalopes Neighbourhood Dive
stops.description: Your neighborhood dive bar. Metalheads, punks, and locals can be seen hanging in the dark drinking a pint at any time of day.
stops.goto: Pints.

stops.name: Obanhmi
stops.description: Obanmi has a few locations across the city, and although you can argue there is lots of good banh-mi for cheaper, the veggie option here is unmatched!
stops.goto: Tofu bahn-mi

stops.name: Plain Jane Ice Cream
stops.description: After a short hiatus, Plain Jane is back! This is ultimately the freshest ice cream you can buy. Made right in front of you with a specialized machine, you choose your berry and you get the best soft serve in the neighborhood.
stops.goto: blueberry!

stops.name: Iron Dog Books
stops.description: A diverse collection of books with lots of options from Canadian and Indigenous voices. A friendly vibe
stops.goto: Look through the Canadian and Indigenous authors section to see what's new.

stops.name: The Narrow Lounge
stops.description: A tiny cocktail bar hidden down in a little corner at the bottom of Main. A good place to get a couple cocktails to start the night or a couple to end the night.
stops.goto: They do any of the classics well!

stops.name: Antisocial Skateboard Shop
stops.description: A neighborhood staple, Antisocial is in a new location, but with the same energy. Stop in for hardware or soft goods needs, check out the magazines and other local products as well. In the spring and summer, you can also find a lovely assortment of flowers and veggies from their local farm.
stops.goto: Shop tee's, hardware, magazines.

stops.name: Earnest Ice Cream | Quebec St.
stops.description: Their Main St. location is super busy all summer long for a reason. Same great ice cream, and a quick walk to the seawall!
stops.goto: COOKIES & CREAM on a waffle cone.

stops.name: Glory Juice Co.
stops.description: If you're looking for a quick, healthy recharge in juice, wrap, or salad format, this is your place.

stops.name: Purebread Bakery + Coffee
stops.description: There is a reason Purebread has multiple locations spanning across Vancouver up to Whistler. Some of the best baked goods in the city.
stops.goto: Cookies & croissants.

stops.name: Mount Pleasant Vintage
stops.description: A fun spot with a great energy. The cocktails are unique and flavorful and the grill gets you thinking its summer all year long. Large plates are great for sharing and you can find yourself here longer than you'd expect (in a good way!).
stops.goto: Duck A L'Orange and some tater tots for the table.

stops.name: Jonathan Rogers Park
stops.description: One of the best spots for late summer evenings, this park has decent mountain views, close access to breweries and restaurants, a bathroom, and overall good vibe.
stops.goto: Set up a blanket, grab a six pack from 33a, play some bocce, and people watch.

stops.name: 33 Acres Brewing Company
stops.description: 33A & B are neighborhood staples with some of the best craft beer in the city. With two neighboring locations, a park nearby, and a large patio, you can find many ways to enjoy what they have to offer. Their food is quite good as well!
stops.goto: Sunshine or darkness.

stops.name: The Federal Store Luncheonette & Grocer
stops.description: A cute spot just off the main area. Great people watching on their little patio that makes you feel like you're in Europe watching all the bicycles zip by.
stops.goto: Americano on the bench.

stops.name: Superbaba (Vancouver)
stops.description: One of my go to spots for take out in this neighborhood. The plates are generous and the wraps are delicious.
stops.goto: Falafel plate.

stops.name: Fox Cabaret
stops.description: You can often find great shows in this venue, all types of diverse events, and an array of cocktails and beer options.
stops.goto: Check their listings for local bands and shows.

stops.name: Brassneck Brewery
stops.description: One of the OG craft breweries in the area, Brassneck serves some of the tastiest brews in the neighborhood. You'll always find something new and fresh on tap.
stops.goto: Magicians Assistant or the Passive Aggressive.

stops.name: Cultivate Tea
stops.description: A highly curated tea house, Cultivate takes the utmost care and pride in their tea. A truly unique tea experience.
stops.goto: Their choice, they never steer me wrong.

stops.name: Como Taperia
stops.description: Easily one of my favorite spots in the neighborhood. If you are looking to share some incredible dishes with friends in a good atmosphere, this is your spot.
stops.goto: Order one (or two) of every dish.

stops.name: Budgies Burritos
stops.description: A neighborhood staple for vegetarians, Budgies has great budget friendly options for everyone. Everything can also be made vegan and gluten free.
stops.goto: The Johnny O

stops.name: F as in Frank Vintage Clothing
stops.description: One of the first vintage shops I ever visited in Vancouver, F as in Frank is a legendary spot with a ton of unique selections including their vintage re-worked pieces. T's, athletic apparel, and the vintage classics can all be found here.

stops.name: Glorydays Fine Goods
stops.description: Glorydays has been in the game for almost a decade, moving from Mintage Mall to their own location in the last couple of years. Specializing in Vintage T's and their popular shop merch.

stops.name: Rath Art Supplies
stops.description: Small shop specializing in art supplies.
stops.goto: Locally made water colour mini-cubes.

stops.name: Gene Coffee Bar
stops.description: One of the best spots in the neighborhood for a coffee view of the mountains. An iconic location with good coffee. Nice spot to start a day or get some work done.
stops.goto: Coffee on the bench out front people watching and taking in mountain views.

stops.name: The Corner Store
stops.description: A Complex store carrying brands such as Asics, Carhartt WIP, Pleasures, Metalwood Studios, Stussy, Nike, as well as some shop exclusives like Mt. Pleasant Sports.

stops.name: Tocador
stops.description: Colourful Cuban restaurant with some exotic cocktails and flavorful dishes. Great for a late night snack and cocktail on the patio.
stops.goto: Bird Conspiracy & a couple comidas.

stops.name: ONIGIRI YA at Maruhachi GAIDEN
stops.description: Stop in for some Maruhachi Tsuke-men or grab a couple onigiri to go. I've been sneaking into this place for years for a quick nourishing meal.
stops.goto: Cha-shu Tsuke-men or a couple Onigiri

stops.name: 49th Parallel Café & Lucky's Doughnuts
stops.description: If you're into coffee, patios, and doughnuts, this is a no brainer for you!

stops.name: Burgoo
stops.description: Relaxed but high quality comfort food. Soups, sandwiches in a rustic and authentic setting.
stops.goto: Lobster bisque or Burgoo burger

stops.name: Urban Source
stops.description: Sometimes, you just need a random test tube, poster, pom pom, or antique picture frame you didn't know you actually needed. This spot is a scrap-bookers dreamland, with tons of stuff to choose from and an ever rotating assortment of new-to-you goods.
stops.goto: Check out what they have out front!

stops.name: Mount Pleasant Park
stops.description: A lovely little community park just off of main that has lots to do.
stops.goto: Grab some goodies from main and come chill in the park. Occasionally shoot a basketball or skate.

stops.name: Sushi Hil
stops.description: If you are after the highest quality and a refined sushi experience, Sushi Hil is for you. A very classic sushi aesthetic, they let their sushi do the talking. Chef's choice is always a win if budget isn't an issue.
stops.goto: Chirashi, or whatever the server recommends that night!

stops.name: The Granville Island Toy Company
stops.description: It's easy to get lost in this shop, with a plethora of games, toys, and activities for all ages. A warning for parents: hard to get your kid to leave!

stops.name: Anh and Chi
stops.description: A long-standing Vietnamese restaurant in the neighborhood, with many dishes that keep you coming back. A nice patio and even nicer interior, great service and excellent menu.
stops.goto: Wok fried noodles or rice.

stops.name: Good Thief
stops.description: Described as the "rebellious sibling" to Anh and Chi, this cocktail bar serves up some truly unique cocktails. I've only been here a couple of times, but I was absolutely blown away by the diversity and uniqueness of the bar offerings
stops.goto: Best Kept Secret

stops.name: Welk's
stops.description: Welk's is a neighborhood swiss army knife. They have almost anything you could possibly need, from greeting cards to kombucha, from mason jars, to door hinges. I find myself going there for the most random items, and they always seem to have what I am looking for!
stops.goto: Anything!

stops.name: Neptoon Records
stops.description: A quirky neighborhood record shop where you can often run into local legends and big time celebs rifling through the stacks.

stops.name: The Sellution Quality Consignment
stops.description: If you are looking for some pre-loved furniture for your home, this is a spot to check out.

stops.name: Woo To See You
stops.description: A cute shop specializing in women's clothing and accessories.

stops.name: Eugene Choo
stops.description: A high end clothing store that has been in the neighborhood a long time. Specializing in locally made and expertly curated pieces.

stops.name: Collage Collage
stops.description: A cute independently owned bookstore that is high curated and tones of fun to browse.
stops.goto: I love grabbing a new book for my daughter any time I am around.

stops.name: Liberty Bakery + Café
stops.description: One of my favorite spots on main to grab a coffee and light snack. In the fall and winter, the interior is warm and cozy, in the spring and summer, you can find me outside with my coffee and cookie.
stops.goto: A slice of quiche, a cookie, and a coffee

stops.name: Exile Guitars
stops.description: One of my favorite shops to browse, especially if you feel like you want to walk into a musical time machine. So many great pieces to look at, and if you are musically inclined, play.

stops.name: The Soap Dispensary and Kitchen Staples
stops.description: If you are looking to get some unique kitchen items, shop waste-free, or sample some soaps and topicals, the Soap dispensary is your stop.
stops.goto: Reusable everything!

stops.name: Front & Company
stops.description: Front & Company is one of those clothing stores that really has something for everyone. With a large selection of consignment clothing, you can find quality goods for all fits.
stops.goto: I always take a look at the shoes.

stops.name: Front & Company: Gift Store
stops.description: A fun little gift shop to get that extra little something you need for a gift.
stops.goto: Peanuts and hello kitty stickers for my kiddo.

stops.name: Kotn
stops.description: This shop offers sustainably sourced materials, with styles designed in Canada. Lots of new styles and basics if you are into high quality fabrics sourced ethically.
stops.goto: Tank tops and tees. Their socks are super comfy too.

stops.name: Trafiq Cafe & Bakery
stops.description: High quality baked goods, cakes, and coffee.
stops.goto: A shot of espresso and a cookie.

stops.name: Las Tortas Mexican Gourmet Sandwiches
stops.description: Authentic Mexican tortas and tacos.
stops.goto: Cochinita tortas.

stops.name: The Shameful Tiki Room
stops.description: Looking for a unique cocktail experience? Shameful Tiki room is for you. Dark, mysterious, and a ton of fun. Service is hilarious and the cocktails are too. A must try if you are looking for a fun late night out.
stops.goto: Volcano bowl (with friends).

stops.name: Red Cat Records
stops.description: Great spot to browse new and used records or sell some of your old collection.

stops.name: Hunter & Hare - Main St
stops.description: Another quality consignment stop on Main st. with great selection for kids and ladies.
stops.goto: I always check the back for kids clothes!

stops.name: East is East
stops.description: Some of the best Indian in the city with a diverse range of dishes. Unbeatable flavors and great service. Great for family style dining.
stops.goto: Silk Route tasting menu

stops.name: Bunker Apparel + Goods
stops.description: As you walk downstairs, you are greeted with a large room of antique furniture which is always fascinating to look through, then, you head towards the back where there is a large collection of vintage clothes of all genres, and to the right, you find my favorite room, full of antiques and oddities.
stops.goto: Flip through the clothes and look through the antiques.

stops.name: Coco et Olive
stops.description: A cute little cafe with a bunch of nice gifts and snacks. A sweet spot to grab a coffee and light lunch and sit outside at the parklet next door.
stops.goto: Latte and a baked good, browse the goods while waiting for my coffee.

stops.name: Thierry Mount Pleasant
stops.description: A very refined experience, Thierry has anyone looking for high quality Parisian baked goods taken care of.
stops.goto: A latte and a croissant.

stops.name: Fish Market Studio
stops.description: This vintage shop specializes in "designer brands" and newer wave vintage trends like y2k. Check out their website for some cool look books and inspo.

stops.name: Pulpfiction Books
stops.description: A great selection of new and used books from a local staple.

stops.name: Mintage Mall
stops.description: Mintage Mall is a collection of vintage sellers upstairs in a historic building. You can get lost in here for hours looking through all the different offerings from many of the cities best pickers.
stops.goto: Do a full lab of the entire place.

stops.name: AJ's Brooklyn Pizza Joint
stops.description: AJs is easily my favorite pizza on Main Street. With so many different pies to choose from and a staff that always is attentive and quick, it's a no brainer this place is ALWAYS busy.
stops.goto: Bar pie!

stops.name: Carp Sushi&Bowl
stops.description: A great spot for a quick roll or a bowl, Carp has some fresh sushi and great flavors
stops.goto: Chirashi or Poke bowl

stops.name: The Lido
stops.description: This cash only locals establishment is a great spot for a late night cocktail. You'll run into an eclectic group of people in this spot.
stops.goto: Bar specials.

stops.name: Prince Edward Park
stops.description: A cute little park just off Main with a  spray park and playground for kids.

stops.name: Dude Chilling
stops.description: The iconic Dude Chilling park, just off main, a perfect spot to throw down the blanket, play some bocce, drink a bottle of wine, and hang out with locals.

stops.name: Riley Park
stops.description: Cute little neighborhood park with washrooms, three separate kids park, and close to Nat Bailey Stadium.

stops.name: Alternative Creations Studio
stops.description: Alternative Creations Studio, located in East Vancouver, is a collective that supports artists with developmental disabilities.
stops.goto: Head into the gallery and view the artwork.

stops.name: Bohemia Gallery Inc.
stops.description: Three vintage shops in a row with more of the eclectic, "hippie" aesthetic. Colorful, wild, and unique.

stops.name: Vancouver Special
stops.description: A high-end contemporary furniture and home goods store.

stops.name: Lykopis Archery
stops.description: An indoor archery range specializing in introductory classes for every level.

stops.name: Lucky's Books & Comics
stops.description: A fun local bookstore with lots of books for kids and adults. Comics in the back!

stops.name: Body Energy Club @ Main Street
stops.description: Sometimes when I've had a long day cruising the neighborhood, I really crave a well-made protein shake. Body energy club is my go to spot!
stops.goto: PB&J Smoothie

stops.name: Elysian Coffee 7th & Ontario
stops.description: Elysian is my go to spot if I need a quick coffee or espresso on the North side of Main St. Lots of baked goods and lunch foods as well.

stops.name: Helen's Grill
stops.description: Helen's is a classic in the neighborhood, a great spot for breakfast, lunch, and early dinners. Serving all the standard breakfast and grill foods. Cash only!
stops.goto: Pancakes or steak & eggs.

stops.name: Ayoub's Dried Fruits and Nuts
stops.description: With many locations in the lower mainland, Ayoub's has a great selection of bulk foods and treats with middle-eastern and Mediterranean flavors.
stops.goto: Dried figs and the Mediterranean spiced cashews.

stops.name: City Centre Artist Lodge
stops.description: A motel turned "artist lodge" City Centre motel now houses many different artist studios. Check out their website for events and open studio days.

stops.name: UNCOMMON
stops.description: Curated vintage shop in the heart of Main street.

stops.name: Say Hey Cafe
stops.description: If you're into sandwiches, this is your place. With so many amazing staples and an ever rotating seasonal menu, Say Hey always has the goods to stay or to go.
stops.goto: Doofer or the crunchy.

stops.name: The Boxcar
stops.description: One of my favorite cocktail bars in the entire city. Inside is dark and moody, great music, and a fun patio if the weather is right. With Mortadella next door, it's a great spot to start or end a night out.
stops.goto: A good company and company or espresso martini.

stops.name: Mortadella
stops.description: Roman style slices and pies with fantastic toppings. A newer pizza joint in the city but one you should not sleep on.
stops.goto: Go with the name sake to start. Potato slice is good too!

stops.name: The American
stops.description: This lively bar can be visited at nearly any time of day if you are looking for some games and beers. They often host fun events so make sure you check the calendar if you're looking for a party. The burgers also make a great side to a fun night out.
stops.goto: Beer, burgers, and pool.

stops.name: Torafuku
stops.description: This modern Asian restaurant always has a diverse menu offering many different, delicious items to choose from. Their cocktails pair very well with all their dishes and if I could come here once a season, I would!
stops.goto: Menu changes seasonally, but I've never left disappointed!

stops.name: Hunnybee Bruncheonette
stops.description: One of the most popular brunch spots in the city for a reason. Always great food and coffee with a wrap around patio for watching the bikes zip by on their way to the seawall.
stops.goto: Pancakes and a coffee.

stops.name: The Program Fitness
stops.description: The Program offers fitness classes as well as personal training with a focus on an entry to fitness for any level.
stops.goto: Suffer through an intense 45 minutes!

stops.name: Gore Street Vintage
stops.description: GSV is one of the OG vintage shops in Chinatown, with a crazy selection of graphic tee's, work wear, and vintage designer, there is something for everyone.

stops.name: Phnom Penh Restaurant
stops.description: Easily one of the most famous spots in Chinatown for their signature dishes, a long standing tradition and loved by all.
stops.goto: Chicken wings, butter beef, meatball noodle soup.

stops.name: Straight Brooklyn Pizza
stops.description: Great spot to grab a fresh slice before heading downtown for a game or concert.
stops.goto: Pesto or Cup N Char

stops.name: London Pub
stops.description: If you are looking for a rowdy crowd to watch a game with, slam a pint of Guiness, and throw some darts, this is your spot. You never know where your night might take you if you end up at the London Pub.

stops.name: Los Sapos Tacos
stops.description: Authentic Mexican street Taco's in Chinatown.
stops.goto: One of each taco.

stops.name: friend of a friend
stops.description: Artist studio housing some of the most unique creatives in the city.

stops.name: Keefer Bar
stops.description: Cocktail bar serving some of the best martinis in the city.
stops.goto: Gin martini with a side of olives.

stops.name: Annabelle's
stops.description: Cute little cafe facing the park on the corner. Amazing and unique pastries, gelato, and delicious coffee.
stops.goto: Coffee and a pastry.

stops.name: The Plaza Skatepark
stops.description: The iconic Plaza Skatepark is always worth a stop if you are in the area. See some of the best skaters in the city in one of the most legendary skate parks in Canada.
stops.goto: Grab a coffee and watch.

stops.name: Between 2 Buns
stops.description: My favorite smash burger spot in the city, hands down.
stops.goto: Single with cheese, no substitutions.

stops.name: DD Mau
stops.description: DD Mau has modern takes on classic Viet dishes. I enjoy going there late night for a cocktail and sharing a few of their signature plates.
stops.goto: Bar Snax, Wings, Fried Sticky Rice, Sate beef sandwich.

stops.name: Pizza Coming Soon
stops.description: A modern Japanese snack bar with a constantly rotating menu, very cool interior, and great atmosphere.
stops.goto: Pina Coming Soon and a mix of whatever is new from the food menu!

stops.name: HMPL
stops.description: HMPL is a bike bag and accessory manufacturer, but also has a storefront with a curated selection of new and used gear.
stops.goto: Browse all of the vintage bike jerseys and mountain bike shorts.

stops.name: Mello
stops.description: If you're looking for a brioche bun with some really wild and fun toppings, Mello is your jam (no pun intended).
stops.goto: Cereal milk donut.

stops.name: Kissa Tanto
stops.description: Italian-Japanese food with an incredible ambiance and menu. One of my most suggested restaurants when people are visiting the city as it's something you truly can't find anywhere else.

stops.name: Bao Bei
stops.description: Modern Chinese with some unique cocktails and dishes. Great for dining with a small group of close friends.

stops.name: New Town Bakery
stops.description: Family owned Hong Kong style bakery/cafe serving some of the best buns and traditional grab and go items in the city.
stops.goto: Coconut bun and a pork bun.

stops.name: Bagheera
stops.description: A "speak-easy" with a fun way to enter (I won't ruin it for you), and a really good selection of fancy cocktails.

stops.name: Propaganda
stops.description: A quiet and unassuming coffee shop in the heart of Chinatown with amazing roasts and quick service.
stops.goto: A cappuccino or shot of espresso.

stops.name: Strathcona Park
stops.description: Large park with huge grassy areas, big trees, a concrete skate park, and a basketball court.

stops.name: La Casa Gelato
stops.description: Home of hundreds of flavors, La Casa Gelato is a spectacle on a hot summer evening. With so many flavors to try, it's a wonder anyone makes it out of there with a cone!
stops.goto: Nutella scoop.

stops.name: Rise Up Marketplace
stops.description: A fun little Jamaican grocer and lunch spot in a heritage building with great vibes and even better food.
stops.goto: A patty to go. Veggie or meat, both are great.

stops.name: Prototype Coffee
stops.description: Some of the most unique micro-roasts you'll ever find in any city. With in house roasting that can be smelt from a block away. Experts at any style of coffee you like.
stops.goto: A pour-over with whatever beans the barista is excited about.

stops.name: The Irish Heather Shebeen
stops.description: For the best pint of Guinness and tasty curry fries, with a side of local hooligans, this is your spot.
stops.goto: In the description!

stops.name: Touski
stops.description: A new, cute, little coffee shop sister to Say Hey Cafe. Great quiet spot during the week to have a coffee and do some light work. Treats are all amazing too.
stops.goto: The cranberry cookie is very unique!

stops.name: Livestock
stops.description: Livestocks Chinatown location has an amazing wall of shoes with a rotating selection of the newest from brands like Adidas, Arc'teryx, Asics, Converse, Jordan, Norda, Puma, Salomon, Timberland and many more.

stops.name: Massy Books
stops.description: This indigenous owned bookstore has a great selection of books and donates to many local charities and organizations with funds generated from sales.

stops.name: De Xing Long Trading Co. LTD
stops.description: Among many of these types of shops, this one in particular has friendly service and fair prices. If it's your first time in Chinatown, it's worth a walk through!

stops.name: MacLean Park
stops.description: Just between Chinatown and Strathcona, this park has one of the best spray pads in the city. Big open area for picnics and large trees for lots of shade. A good stop if you're touring Chinatown or Strathcona with kids.
